message:
feat(wait): tick 209 wait_for_cx honors cancel during loop and backoff (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Continuation of the tick-208 bug fix. `wait::wait_for_cx` had the
same anti-pattern as retry.rs before tick 208:

  let _ = crate::runtime_compat::sleep_with_cx(cx, sleep_for).await;

and no top-of-loop cancel check. Net effect: once a wait_for_cx
loop started, cancel fired on the caller's cx was swallowed and
the predicate kept being polled until the natural timeout or
retries-exhausted condition fired.

Tick 209 fixes wait_for_cx:

1. Top-of-loop `cx.is_cancel_requested()` check. Returns WaitError
   with `last_observed = Some("cancelled")` so callers can
   distinguish cancel from natural timeout (the error type itself
   stays for API stability — `elapsed < timeout` is also a signal
   of premature return).

2. `sleep_with_cx` result is checked. On Err (cancel during
   backoff), returns the same WaitError-with-"cancelled" marker.

Because `wait_for_quiescence_cx` and `wait_for_quiescence_with_backoff_cx`
both route through `wait_for_cx` (L403 / L430), the fix propagates
transitively.

Legacy `wait_for_inner` (L224) non-cx path unchanged.

Verification: 116 / 116 wait:: tests pass under
--features asupersync-runtime via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang,
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target), including:
- wait_for_condition_times_out
- wait_for_max_retries_exhausted
- wait_for_predicate_succeeds_after_n_attempts
- quiescence_producer_consumer_eventually_quiet

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
